What if we put a price on food's carbon?

Drag the dial to set a global greenhouse-gas price and watch how the world's croplands, diets, animal feed and emissions reorganise as the GLADE model re-optimises the food system. placeholder data

0 USD / t CO2-eq
Cropland use colour = main crop group · fade = cropland per land area
Pasture & grazing land shade = pasture per land area
lessmore
Net food-system emissions
0Gt CO2-eq / yr
System cost USD trillion / yr · the three components summed
Global diet g / person / day · plant-based → animal-based
Animal feed Mt dry matter / yr · by source

Fixed vs flexible diet. Under fixed diet, consumption is pinned to the 2020 baseline; production, land use, animal feed and emissions still re-optimise as the price rises. Under flexible diet, the diet also re-optimises, shifting in response to the carbon price alone (health is left unvalued).

Net emissions. All greenhouse gases (CO2, CH4, N2O) as CO2-equivalent, including land-use change and sequestration credits from spared land.

Diet & feed. Diet is the global-average per-capita intake, shown by mass (g/person/day) or food energy (kcal/person/day) via the toggle; animal feed is total dry matter (Mt/yr) by source.

System cost. The model objective, split into three terms that sum to it: production cost (growing, processing, trade and supplying food); resistance to change (penalties for deviating from observed production, plus -- under flexible diet -- the loss of consumer value relative to the starting diet); and the social cost of carbon (the carbon price times net emissions, which turns negative as the system reaches net-negative emissions and earns sequestration credits).

Maps. Cropland is coloured by its dominant crop group and faded by cropland area per unit land; pasture is shaded by grazing-land area per unit land.

Data. The GLADE paper's published fixed-diet carbon-price sweep (Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.20617942); the dial interpolates between solved scenarios.

GLADE · global food-system optimisation
